name: OntoKG
maintainer: ProRata.ai — authors: Yitao Li, Zhanlin Liu, Anuranjan Pandey, Muni Srikanth
license: [uncertain] — code at github.com/Prorata-ai/OntoKG; license file not confirmed from available sources
last_major_release: [uncertain] — arXiv preprint 2604.02618 submitted 2026-04-03; no versioned software release identified
activity: [uncertain] — single arXiv preprint (April 2026); GitHub repo Prorata-ai/OntoKG exists but stars/forks/commits not confirmed from search results
storage_backend: backend-portable property graph — declarative schema exports to any labeled property graph store (Neo4j, Postgres, etc.); Wikidata dump is data source, not runtime store
representation_primitive: entity + typed edge (labeled property graph). Intrinsic modules map entity properties to typed node attributes (tabular, for lookup). Relational modules map properties to directed typed edges (traversable). Output: 34.0M nodes, 61.2M edges, 38 relationship types from 94 schema modules (56 intrinsic, 38 relational) across 8 ontology categories.
api_shape: [uncertain] — research artifact with GitHub schema files; no runtime Python library, REST API, CLI, or first-party MCP server identified
hosting: self-host — schema and construction code on GitHub; no managed cloud offering
llm_dependency: ingest only — LLM used as tool-augmented assistant during schema construction for property classification and validation. Downstream querying of exported graph requires no LLM. Specific LLM client not named in the paper.
schema_extensibility: intrinsic-relational-routing — the core contribution. Every Wikidata property classified iteratively as intrinsic (node attribute) or relational (edge) via rule-based routing assigned to one of 94 modules across 8 categories. Schema is designed to be reusable and portable.
schema_richness: 38 relational edge types (one per relational module); 56 intrinsic node-attribute modules; 8 top-level ontology categories. Instantiated on Wikidata: 34.0M nodes, 61.2M edges. 93.3% category coverage, 98.0% module assignment. The 38 edge types exceeds Gimle §5's 15+ edge target. Multi-label and multi-graph not discussed — single-domain (Wikidata). Gap: no software-project entity types (Symbol, File, PR, Decision, Iteration).
temporal_model: none — instantiated as static snapshot of January 2026 Wikidata dump; no bi-temporal validity windows, event-log semantics, or recorded_at timestamps
forgetting_policy: none — static construction from fixed dump; no incremental update or eviction mechanism
ingest_latency_class: batch-offline — processes 100M-item Wikidata dump via iterative rule-based routing with LLM augmentation; hours-scale construction
retrieval_modes: graph-traversal — paper uses live SPARQL during schema construction for exploratory inspection; exported property graph supports Cypher or SPARQL per backend; no vector, BM25, or hybrid retrieval described
retrieval_agenticity: [uncertain] — paper proposes schema-guided retrieval for RAG as a next step but does not implement it; no multi-hop or agentic retrieval described
multi_project_namespace: none natively — single-domain schema (Wikidata); domain customization (one of five validated applications) allows sub-graph scoping by category/module filter, not a first-class namespace mechanism
multi_user_access: none — research artifact; no access control model
embedder_options: none — no vector embeddings; entity disambiguation via typed schema structure and ontology, not embedding similarity
llm_client_options: used at construction time only for property classification; specific client unspecified; rule-based routing can operate without LLM for many properties
cost_privacy: Data stays on-premise (Wikidata dump processed locally). LLM calls incurred only at schema construction; exact token cost not published. No per-query LLM cost after schema is built.
code_artifact_linkage: none — OntoKG targets encyclopedic knowledge (Wikidata); no code-specific entity types (File, Symbol, Function, PR, Commit) in the schema
code_analysis_features: none — not applicable; paper scope is general-purpose KG construction from encyclopedic structured data
memory_os_layer: plaintext — property graph stores facts; no parametric (weight-tuning) or activation (KV-cache) layer
self_improvement_mechanism: none — static construction; schema is manually curated with LLM assistance but does not self-update from query feedback or execution traces
provenance_standard: none described — no audit trail, OpenTelemetry, or W3C-PROV instrumentation mentioned
benchmark_results: No LongMemEval/LoCoMo/SWE-bench results (not applicable). Schema quality: 93.3% category coverage on 34.6M-entity core set; 98.0% module assignment among classified entities. Validated across 5 downstream applications: ontology structure analysis, benchmark annotation auditing, entity disambiguation, domain customization, LLM-guided extraction.
strengths: 1. Intrinsic-relational routing provides the most principled published answer to the property-vs-edge decision — directly applicable to Gimle schema design for eliminating spurious edges. 2. Declarative schema is backend-portable and reusable independently of the construction pipeline. 3. Validated at 34M-node / 61M-edge scale with 93.3% coverage, proving the routing heuristic generalizes.
weaknesses: 1. Domain is Wikidata-specific; software project entities (Symbol, PR, Decision, Iteration, UIComponent, APIEndpoint) are entirely absent and require full domain re-engineering. 2. No temporal model — Gimle requires bi-temporal (valid_from/to + recorded_at) which OntoKG does not address. 3. No runtime interface (no Python lib, no MCP server, no REST API) — purely a schema artifact and construction pipeline.
fit_with_gimle_section_5: Verdict: steal-specific-ideas — the intrinsic-relational routing pattern is the single most relevant contribution for Gimle N+1 schema design. Key ideas to steal: (1) Routing heuristic: if a property answers 'what is this entity?' it is intrinsic (node attribute); if it answers 'how does this entity relate to another?' it is relational (edge). Applied to Gimle: File.language, Symbol.signature, Decision.rationale, Iteration.status are intrinsic; CALLS, IMPORTS, RESOLVES_TO, OWNS, DECIDES_IN, MODIFIES are relational. (2) Module taxonomy: assigning each relation to exactly one typed module eliminates catch-all RELATES_TO edges — maps directly to Gimle's goal of avoiding spurious edges. (3) Size reference: 38 strongly-typed edges at 34M-node / 61M-edge scale validates that 15+ typed edges is achievable and sufficient for Gimle §5. Schema snippet (Gimle adaptation): Intrinsic (node attrs): Project.name, File.path, Symbol.kind, Decision.rationale, Iteration.closed_at. Relational (edges): (:File)-[:DEFINES]->(:Symbol), (:Symbol)-[:CALLS]->(:Symbol), (:Decision)-[:APPLIES_TO]->(:Module). Not adoptable whole: no temporal model, no software entities, no runtime API.
